field_values.action_summary: Dorothy finds an oil-can in the Woodman's cottage. She lubricates his joints, restoring his movement. The Woodman awakens and expresses his desire to seek Oz for a heart.
field_values.emotional_beat: From tension to relief and newfound companionship.
field_values.camera_intent: Close-ups of oil application to mechanical joints; Medium shots of jerking limb movements during restoration; Reaction shots of dorothy and the_scarecrow
field_values.end_state: scene resolves into the next narrative beat
movement_notes: Visible sheen or wetness of oil on tin surfaces
Progression of joint movement from rigid to fluid
field_values.transition_notes:
# Scene Contract: CH005_SC003

## Production Intent
Resolve the physical immobilization of the Tin Woodman through lubrication, transitioning the group dynamic from a rescue mission to an expanded traveling party.

## Required Cast
- dorothy (young human girl, weary traveler, white dress)
- the_scarecrow (companion)
- tin_woodman (metal/tin character, initially rusted/immobile)

## Required Environments
- forest_and_cottage_setting (specifically cottage_interior)

## Continuity Constraints
- The "wetness" or sheen of the oil on the tin surfaces must be consistent during application and movement.
- Mechanical movement must transition from jerking/staccato to fluid as lubrication progresses.

## Beat List
- BT001: Discovery of the oil-can in the cottage.
- BT002: Application of oil to the Tin Woodman's joints and restoration of movement.
- BT003: The Tin Woodman awakens and declares his quest for a heart.
field_values.dialogue_relevance: dialogue supports scene intent and continuity
